prose:
Method provenance block – frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap
Property: Kohn–Sham HOMO and LUMO orbital energies and resulting HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: single-point on fixed input geometry provided by the user (benzene.xyz, 12 atoms) – no geometry optimisation (notation: DFT//input)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF backend; command shown in CLI invocation of result JSON (see below)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine (`warnings` array empty). Integrity check status "ok" with trustworthy = true. Density fitting (RI/JK) is an approximation to two-electron integrals and is on by default in PySCF.

Key numerical results (gas-phase Kohn–Sham orbital energies)
  HOMO energy  = –7.0468329 eV
  LUMO energy  = –0.41574728 eV
⇒ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV

Reproducibility
CLI invocation (from result JSON):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphkk7ctm8/live.json
Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphkk7ctm8/live.json
The engine streamed a live log (.out) to the caller’s working directory while running; its path is available in the session history and can be inspected with e.g. `tail -f` for full SCF details.

No warnings were issued by the backend (warnings array empty).
